page: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD EPYC processor with 48 shared vCPUs, derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled operating at a base speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include a GPU.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is highly efficient, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths reaching top-tier status, while cached small-block read bandwidth ranks in the top 25%. For database operations, the system achieves strong results in Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring high memory capacity and substantial NVMe storage without requiring dedicated, isolated CPU cores. This resource density makes it highly suitable for large-scale memory-intensive applications, database hosting, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference tasks, where its strong memory bandwidth and large local storage capacity can be fully utilized.
description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 memory-optimized server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz across 24 physical cores, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and SHA extensions. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU execution and uncached memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing and database performance. While the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, the high memory-to-core ratio and integrated NVMe storage provide a cost-effective platform for memory-intensive workloads. This configuration is highly suited for database hosting, large-scale caching, static web serving, and memory-heavy data processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 server with 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density shared CPU server combining massive memory capacity and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory.
2880 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and uncached memory bandwidth.
Strong multi-core processing and database operation throughput.
Well-suited for database hosting, caching, and memory-intensive workloads.
